title: Complete Guide to Macrame Cords
excerpt: Everything you need to know about choosing the right cord for your macrame projects.

steps.title: Cotton cord types
steps.description: Single strand is great for fringe, while twisted rope holds knots better and creates defined patterns.

steps.title: Choosing thickness
steps.description: 1-2mm for jewelry, 3-4mm for small decor, 5-6mm for wall hangings, 8mm+ for large projects.

steps.title: Natural vs synthetic
steps.description: Cotton is most popular for its softness. Jute adds rustic charm. Nylon works for outdoor items.

steps.title: Calculating amounts
steps.description: General rule: multiply your finished length by 4-5x to account for knotting.

tips: Buy extra cord - it's better to have leftovers than run short
Store cord in a dry place to prevent mildew
